quiz.title: Accounting and Month-End Quiz
quiz.description: Test your understanding of accounting basics, month end and Mayday product workflows.

questions.question: When finance people talk about debits and credits, what are they mainly referring to?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The two ways cash can move in and out of a bank account

answers.label: B
answers.text: The two main categories shown on a profit and loss report

answers.label: C
answers.text: The two sides of an accounting entry

answers.label: D
answers.text: The balances owed by customers and to suppliers

questions.question: What is the main rule of double-entry bookkeeping?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Total debits must equal total credits

answers.label: B
answers.text: Every transaction is recorded in a single account

answers.label: C
answers.text: Every transaction must be reviewed by an accountant

answers.label: D
answers.text: Every transaction must be settled in cash

questions.question: What does the P&L show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: What the business owns and owes at a single point in time

answers.label: B
answers.text: The cash actually received and paid during the period

answers.label: C
answers.text: How the company's ownership and shares are structured

answers.label: D
answers.text: Income, costs and profit over a period

questions.question: What does a profit margin show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Total revenue left in the bank at month end

answers.label: B
answers.text: Total assets remaining after subtracting liabilities

answers.label: C
answers.text: Profit as a percentage of revenue

answers.label: D
answers.text: The proportion of revenue still owed by customers

questions.question: What does the balance sheet show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The income, costs and profit the company generated during the month

answers.label: B
answers.text: What a company owns, owes and what belongs to owners at a point in time

answers.label: C
answers.text: The cash received and paid across the company's bank accounts

answers.label: D
answers.text: The sales the company expects to generate in future periods

questions.question: Why is a cash flow statement different from the P&L?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The P&L tracks cash as it moves, while the cash flow statement focuses on profit

answers.label: B
answers.text: The two reports cover the same figures but are formatted for different audiences

answers.label: C
answers.text: The cash flow statement only records tax and loan payments, unlike the P&L

answers.label: D
answers.text: The P&L shows income and costs by accounting period, while cash flow shows actual cash movements

questions.question: What is the difference between solvency and liquidity?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Solvency is about long-term financial health; liquidity is about short-term cash availability

answers.label: B
answers.text: Solvency measures total sales volume; liquidity measures the profit left afterwards

answers.label: C
answers.text: Solvency tracks tax owed to the authorities; liquidity tracks the cost of running payroll

answers.label: D
answers.text: Solvency looks at how fast invoices are paid; liquidity looks at supplier credit terms

questions.question: What does the accruals principle say?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Income and costs should be recorded only when cash actually changes hands

answers.label: B
answers.text: Income and costs should be brought together and recorded at year end

answers.label: C
answers.text: Income and costs should be recorded in the period they relate to

answers.label: D
answers.text: Income should be recorded whenever a customer settles their invoice

questions.question: Which situation is an example of an accrual?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Paying upfront for a 12-month software subscription

answers.label: B
answers.text: Receiving customer payment before delivering the service

answers.label: C
answers.text: Buying equipment and using it across five years

answers.label: D
answers.text: Receiving legal work in June but getting the invoice in July

questions.question: Which situation is an example of a prepayment?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Completing work for a customer one month but raising the invoice the next

answers.label: B
answers.text: Paying annual insurance upfront and spreading the cost over 12 months

answers.label: C
answers.text: Owing a supplier for goods that were delivered earlier in the period

answers.label: D
answers.text: Receiving cash from a customer well before the service is delivered

questions.question: What is the best distinction between bookkeeping and accounting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Bookkeeping forecasts future results; accounting records the day-to-day transactions

answers.label: B
answers.text: Bookkeeping prepares the tax return; accounting only processes supplier invoices

answers.label: C
answers.text: Bookkeeping records transactions; accounting interprets, adjusts and reports the numbers

answers.label: D
answers.text: Bookkeeping sets the budget; accounting simply enters data into the ledger

questions.question: What is month end?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The recurring process of finalising the accounts for a month

answers.label: B
answers.text: The one-off process of closing the accounts when a company shuts down

answers.label: C
answers.text: The deadline by which all customer invoices must be settled

answers.label: D
answers.text: The yearly process of preparing and filing statutory accounts

questions.question: What is year end?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The process of submitting the company's annual tax return to the authorities

answers.label: B
answers.text: The process of preparing a budget for the upcoming financial year

answers.label: C
answers.text: The process of distributing year-end profits to shareholders

answers.label: D
answers.text: The process of closing the accounts for the financial year

questions.question: What is a journal in accounting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A formal request for payment sent to a customer

answers.label: B
answers.text: An accounting entry posted directly into the ledger

answers.label: C
answers.text: A statement summarising a company's bank transactions

answers.label: D
answers.text: A summary report prepared for the company's shareholders

questions.question: What is depreciation?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Spreading the cost of a fixed asset over its useful life

answers.label: B
answers.text: Writing off the full cost of an asset in the year it is bought

answers.label: C
answers.text: Increasing an asset's value on the balance sheet as it rises over time

answers.label: D
answers.text: Setting cash aside to replace an asset in the future

questions.question: What is an invoice?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A record confirming a payment has already been received

answers.label: B
answers.text: A summary of the profit earned during the month

answers.label: C
answers.text: A document requesting payment for goods or services

answers.label: D
answers.text: A statement matching the ledger to the bank

questions.question: What is an audit?

answers.label: A
answers.text: An internal estimate of the company's future sales performance

answers.label: B
answers.text: A formal request sent to customers asking them to pay

answers.label: C
answers.text: A review carried out by management to set departmental budgets

answers.label: D
answers.text: An independent review of a company's financial statements and supporting records

questions.question: What are management accounts used for?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Meeting the company's legal annual reporting obligations

answers.label: B
answers.text: Internal reporting and decision-making

answers.label: C
answers.text: Providing audited figures for external stakeholders

answers.label: D
answers.text: Calculating and submitting the company's tax returns

questions.question: What are statutory accounts?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Official financial statements prepared to meet legal and regulatory requirements

answers.label: B
answers.text: Internal updates prepared regularly to guide day-to-day decisions

answers.label: C
answers.text: Performance dashboards used by teams to track their targets

answers.label: D
answers.text: Flexible internal reports designed to help managers run the business

questions.question: What is the main difference between management accounts and statutory accounts?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Management accounts are prepared for regulators; statutory accounts are prepared for managers

answers.label: B
answers.text: Management accounts are required by law; statutory accounts are entirely optional

answers.label: C
answers.text: Management accounts cover tax only; statutory accounts cover cash only

answers.label: D
answers.text: Management accounts help run the business; statutory accounts meet formal reporting requirements

questions.question: What does EBITDA stand for?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Earnings Before Income, Tax, Debt and Assets

answers.label: B
answers.text: Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation and Amortisation

answers.label: C
answers.text: Equity Before Interest, Tax, Dividends and Assets

answers.label: D
answers.text: Estimated Business Income, Tax, Debt and Amortisation

questions.question: Which statement best describes variable costs or cost of sales?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Costs that stay broadly the same regardless of sales volume

answers.label: B
answers.text: Costs that relate to long-term financing and interest

answers.label: C
answers.text: Costs that usually increase when the business sells more

answers.label: D
answers.text: Costs recorded on the balance sheet rather than the P&L

questions.question: What does FP&A focus on?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Budgeting, forecasting, analysis and planning

answers.label: B
answers.text: Recording and processing day-to-day supplier bills

answers.label: C
answers.text: Preparing and filing the company's statutory accounts

answers.label: D
answers.text: Chasing and collecting outstanding customer payments

questions.question: What is one key difference between a company and a partnership?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A partnership is typically owned by just one person

answers.label: B
answers.text: A company's owners are personally responsible for all its debts

answers.label: C
answers.text: A partnership is required to publish its accounts publicly

answers.label: D
answers.text: A company is usually a separate legal entity owned by shareholders

questions.question: What is the difference between a branch and a subsidiary?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A branch is a separate legal entity, while a subsidiary is part of the parent

answers.label: B
answers.text: A subsidiary shares the same legal identity as its parent company

answers.label: C
answers.text: A branch is an extension of the same legal entity; a subsidiary is a separate legal entity

answers.label: D
answers.text: A branch is owned by shareholders, while a subsidiary is owned by the parent's customers

questions.question: What does an accounting qualification show?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Someone has been appointed as a director of the company

answers.label: B
answers.text: Someone has formal training and assessment in accounting or finance

answers.label: C
answers.text: Someone is authorised to sign off the statutory accounts alone

answers.label: D
answers.text: Someone specialises exclusively in preparing tax returns

questions.question: What is tax?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Money paid voluntarily to support public services

answers.label: B
answers.text: A charge added by suppliers to their invoices

answers.label: C
answers.text: A fee charged by banks on company accounts

answers.label: D
answers.text: Money paid to government under law

questions.question: What is VAT, GST or sales tax?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A tax charged on sales of goods or services

answers.label: B
answers.text: A tax charged on a company's annual profits

answers.label: C
answers.text: A tax deducted from employees' wages

answers.label: D
answers.text: A tax applied only to goods bought from overseas

questions.question: What is PAYE or PAYG for employment tax purposes?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A system where employees file and pay their own income tax directly

answers.label: B
answers.text: A scheme for reclaiming VAT on business expenses

answers.label: C
answers.text: A system where employers deduct employment tax through payroll and pay it to the tax authority

answers.label: D
answers.text: A method for calculating pension contributions for staff

questions.question: What does gearing measure?

answers.label: A
answers.text: How efficiently a business converts sales into profit

answers.label: B
answers.text: How much a business relies on debt compared with equity

answers.label: C
answers.text: How quickly a business collects cash from its customers

answers.label: D
answers.text: How much cash a business holds in reserve

questions.question: What are financial controls?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Limits placed on how much each department is allowed to spend

answers.label: B
answers.text: Estimates used to predict the company's future sales

answers.label: C
answers.text: Rules that determine when customers must pay their invoices

answers.label: D
answers.text: Processes and checks used to protect the accuracy and reliability of financial information

questions.question: What is the Chart of Accounts?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The structured list of accounts used to categorise financial transactions

answers.label: B
answers.text: A directory of the company's customers and suppliers

answers.label: C
answers.text: A forecast of the company's expected cash flows

answers.label: D
answers.text: A breakdown of the bank accounts held by the business

questions.question: What are reporting dimensions?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Fixed categories required by law on every set of statutory accounts

answers.label: B
answers.text: The range of currencies a company trades and reports in

answers.label: C
answers.text: Labels added to transactions so finance teams can analyse performance by department, location, project or similar categories

answers.label: D
answers.text: The levels of approval needed before a journal can be posted

questions.question: What is consolidated reporting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Merging several departments' budgets within a single entity

answers.label: B
answers.text: Combining multiple entities' financial results into one group-level view

answers.label: C
answers.text: Summarising one entity's results across several months

answers.label: D
answers.text: Comparing a company's results against its competitors

questions.question: What are eliminations in consolidation?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Adjustments that remove transactions and balances between entities in the same group

answers.label: B
answers.text: Adjustments that remove duplicate entries within a single entity

answers.label: C
answers.text: Adjustments that strip one-off items out of the group's results

answers.label: D
answers.text: Adjustments that convert each entity's balances into the group currency

questions.question: What broad problem does Mayday help finance teams solve?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Automating and controlling recurring month-end workflows

answers.label: B
answers.text: Replacing a company's core accounting ledger system

answers.label: C
answers.text: Managing customer relationships and the sales pipeline

answers.label: D
answers.text: Running payroll and filing employee tax returns

questions.question: What do Deferred Revenue and Prepayments both help with?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Spreading income or costs into the correct accounting periods

answers.label: B
answers.text: Calculating the tax due on each customer invoice

answers.label: C
answers.text: Reconciling bank transactions across the group

answers.label: D
answers.text: Forecasting the group's cash flow for the year ahead

questions.question: What does Flux help finance teams do?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Compare month-on-month P&Ls and add variance commentary

answers.label: B
answers.text: Reconcile intercompany balances across entities

answers.label: C
answers.text: Post and review recurring accrual journals

answers.label: D
answers.text: Allocate recharges back to each entity

questions.question: Which statement best describes Recharger, Balancer and BRAG?

answers.label: A
answers.text: They help with multi-entity and intercompany month-end workflows

answers.label: B
answers.text: They all automate revenue recognition schedules

answers.label: C
answers.text: They all compare P&L variances month on month

answers.label: D
answers.text: They all manage the chart of accounts across entities

questions.question: Which pairing is correct?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Mayday HQ helps manage group-wide accounting structures; Close Management helps organise tasks, reviews and close progress

answers.label: B
answers.text: Mayday HQ tracks month-end tasks and reviews; Close Management manages the chart of accounts across entities

answers.label: C
answers.text: Mayday HQ automates intercompany recharges; Close Management reconciles intercompany balances

answers.label: D
answers.text: Mayday HQ recognises deferred revenue; Close Management posts prepayment release schedules
